Title: Form One student critical after fall from school building in Ipoh
Article:
IPOH: A Form One male student is reported to be in critical condition after falling from a building at a secondary school here today.
Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairudin Abu Hanipah has ordered a full report into the incident involving the student.
Khairudin said he was informed of the incident at 4.30pm and immediately went to the Emergency Department of Hospital Raja Permaisuri Bainun (HRPB) after completing a meeting to obtain preliminary information on the case.
He said he also met the victim's parents and conveyed his sympathy to the family.
"I had the opportunity to speak with both parents and express my deepest sympathy over the incident.
"My visit was also accompanied by Education Ministry director-general Datuk Dr Azman Adnan," he said in a Facebook post today.
Khairudin said he had instructed the Kinta District Education Office and the Perak Education Department to prepare a comprehensive report on the incident.
He also directed all schools to remain vigilant and pay close attention to safety conditions within their premises to prevent similar incidents from recurring.
"I have instructed all school authorities to be more alert to their surroundings and ensure that safety measures are always in place so that such incidents do not happen again," he said.
Meanwhile, Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin, when contacted, confirmed the incident and said the cause was still under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Abdul Aziz Hassan remembered for integrity, strong newsroom standards
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former New Straits Times journalist Abdul Aziz Hassan is remembered as a principled and exacting newsroom figure who left a lasting mark on Malaysian journalism.
Paying tribute to the veteran journalist, blogger and former editor Datuk Ahirudin Attan, better known as Rocky Bru, described Abdul Aziz as a stickler for accuracy who was uncompromising when it came to both language and facts.
"Aziz was a stickler for accuracy, both in words and facts, to the point that colleagues did get offended. But Aziz, or Abang Aziz to his reporters at The Mole — the last news portal he worked for — would keep offending them because he felt that was how people learn," he told the New Straits Times.
Ahirudin said Abdul Aziz firmly believed in journalistic independence and stood firm on editorial ethics, including in matters involving publication supplements.
Former journalist Zaidi Azmi remembered Abdul Aziz as a loud, opinionated and headstrong editor who played a significant role in shaping younger reporters.
He said Abdul Aziz had little tolerance for poorly written copy and often took the time to explain mistakes to ensure journalists improved their writing and reporting skills.
He said Abdul Aziz believed journalism could only thrive through proper mentorship and the passing on of knowledge, encouraging reporters to pursue distinctive stories, pay attention to detail and provide readers with the context needed to understand an issue.
Despite his gruff exterior, Zaidi added Abdul Aziz cared deeply about nurturing young talent and remained generous with his time and guidance.
One lesson that remained with him was Abdul Aziz's advice on newswriting.
"Newswriting is like telling a story to your friend. To keep your friend from ignoring you, you have to start with the most interesting part first," Zaidi said.
Former New Straits Times journalist
Abdul Aziz Hassan
died this morning. He was 71.
A Mass Communications graduate from Universiti Teknologi Mara (then ITM), Abdul Aziz was known for his contributions to journalism.
His remains were brought to Masjid Al-Azim in Pandan Indah before burial at the Pandan Indah Muslim cemetery.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pick-up, jeep owners can apply for extra 100 litres under Budi Diesel
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Owners of diesel pick-up trucks and jeeps can apply for an additional 100 litres of subsidised fuel per month, bringing their total monthly quota to 300 litres under the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) mechanism.
Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the allowance is aimed at supporting small and medium enterprise (SME) operators and those living in rural areas who require higher fuel consumption for daily activities.
He said the 300-litre limit is expected to be sufficient, as data from the Finance Ministry shows 95 per cent of diesel users in this category consume less than that amount monthly.
For other eligible private diesel vehicle owners, a base limit of 200 litres per month has been set.
[[nid:1470065]]
"This approach ensures subsidies are given to eligible recipients based on their actual usage, regardless of whether the fuel used is diesel or RON95. It also gives recipients flexibility to manage their fuel use according to their monthly needs," he said at a press conference at the Finance Ministry today.
Recipients can use their quota flexibly between diesel and RON95 depending on their remaining balance.
"Nearly 90 per cent of Malaysian drivers use less than 200 litres of fuel a month, making the base limit adequate for the majority of the 700,000 projected eligible owners.
"The reduction in subsidised diesel prices is one way these savings are channelled back to the people. This initiative is also bolstered by cost-of-living aid like Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR)," he said.
The RM2.10 per litre subsidised rate has also been extended to rural river boat operators and generator set owners.
Owners can check their eligibility and apply for the additional 100-litre pick-up allowance from today, June 22, via the official portal or in person at IRB and UTC offices nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Singaporean driver escapes unhurt as MPV catches fire on PLUS highway
Article:
MUAR: A Singaporean escaped unhurt after the multi-purpose vehicle he was driving was almost completely destroyed in a blaze at Km100.1 of the North-South Expressway (southbound) here this morning.
Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Husaini Eddy Mustafa said the man's Renault Grand Scenic was found to be 95 per cent destroyed upon firefighters' arrival.
"He managed to exit the vehicle safely and was not injured in the incident," Husaini said in a statement today.
Operations were brought under control at 12.07pm after firefighters confirmed there were no remaining hazards.
No other vehicles or motorists were involved in the blaze.
Authorities have yet to determine the cause of the fire.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN Supreme Council holds emergency meeting tonight
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) leaders have gathered at the Pas headquarters here tonight for an emergency Supreme Council meeting.
PN chairman and Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin arrived at the party's headquarters at 8.28pm, accompanied by Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu, Women's chief Datuk Nolee Ashilin Mohammed Radzi and Armada chief Muhammad Hilman Idham.
The meeting, scheduled to begin at 8.30pm, is understood to focus on matters concerning the coalition's component parties as well as preparations for the state polls.
According to a notice dated June 18 and signed by PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, the meeting was convened under Clause 10 (Notice of Meetings and Agenda) and Clause 12 (Frequency of Meetings) of the coalition's constitution.
Among the issues expected to be discussed is the use of the coalition's name.
Takiyuddin had previously said that any activity or meeting using the PN name could not be carried out without the approval of PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar.
He said the directive was aimed at ensuring that all party activities, administrative affairs and management matters were conducted in accordance with the coalition's constitution and complied with the Societies Act 1966.
The status of Parti Wawasan Negara is also expected to be raised following an announcement by Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, who is also the Larut member of parliament, as Opposition Leader during the RESET Malaysia Convention held recently at the Kota Harmoni Expo Site in Tanah Merah, Kelantan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: News@9: Today's top headlines - June 22, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Here are today's top stories:
Populist politicians
Regent of Johor Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim has taken a swipe at
populist politicians
who prioritise social media theatrics over governance, with his remarks widely interpreted as a jab at Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman.
Tougher penalties
The Road Transport (Amendment) Act 2026, which seeks to
strengthen regulatory and enforcement powers
under the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333), was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today.
Trial begins
Two married couples linked to a viral video showing the
alleged abuse of their domestic helpers
claimed trial in separate magistrates' courts here today to charges of causing hurt, criminal intimidation and unlawful possession of passports.
Resignation
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer
announced his resignation
on Monday after fewer than two years in office in a term characterised by policy U-turns and deep public unpopularity.
That's all for News@9.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four-vehicle crash outside Istana Bukit Serene leaves three people injured
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Three people were injured in a multi-vehicle collision involving three cars and a motorcycle in front of the main gate of Istana Bukit Serene here this morning.
The 8.20am accident involved three cars, including a Volkswagen Jetta, and a motorcycle.
Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Abd Wahab Nasir said firefighters found a female victim trapped inside one of the cars, while the others managed to get out of their vehicles.
One driver escaped unhurt, while the Volkswagen driver and the motorcyclist sustained injuries in the crash.
Firefighters carried out rescue operations to extricate the trapped woman before administering initial treatment to all injured victims.
Wahab said the accident scene was declared safe at 9.18am after checks confirmed there were no further hazards.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Theta Edge chairman steps down
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Theta Edge Bhd chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rahman Mamat has resigned to pursue other commitments.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the 74-year-old stepped down from his role as non-independent non-executive chairman.
Theta Edge said there were no disagreements between Abdul Rahman and the board.
He is also associated with MCE Holdings Bhd and ECA Integrated Solution Bhd, according to the filing.
The company did not provide details on a successor to the role.
According to Theta Edge's 2025 annual report, Abdul Rahmat has more than 35 years of experience in public service.
It was largely with the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry, where he served from 1975 until his retirement in 2010 as secretary-general.
Theta Edge closed at 56.5 sen on Monday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM66.65 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nine foreigners suspected of online scam activities detained in Pengkalan Kubor
Article:
TUMPAT: Nine foreigners of various nationalities suspected of entering Malaysia to engage in online scam activities were arrested in Pengkalan Kubor here this morning.
Also detained was a 25-year-old Thai national believed to be a tekong darat responsible for transporting the migrants.
General Operations Force Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspects were detained during Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at Jalan Kampung Kubang Bemban.
"A GOF patrol team from the Eighth Battalion spotted a suspicious Honda Accord travelling along a village road in the area at about 6.30am.
"Upon noticing the presence of the enforcement team, the driver attempted to flee by reversing the vehicle before losing control and crashing into nearby bushes.
"The suspect then locked himself inside the vehicle, forcing officers to break one of the windows before apprehending him after a brief struggle," he said.
Ahmad Radzi said checks on the vehicle uncovered nine migrants comprising six Chinese men, one Chinese woman, one Myanmar man and one Myanmar woman.
"Only one of the migrants was able to produce a Chinese passport, but it did not bear a valid Malaysian entry stamp.
"The others failed to produce any valid travel documents and are believed to have entered the country through illegal routes," he said.
[[nid:1470052]]
Ahmad Radzi said officers also seized 22 mobile phones, a Dell laptop, a laptop bag, a Chinese passport and a Thai identity card.
"Based on the number of mobile phones and electronic devices found in their possession, we suspect the migrants entered Malaysia to carry out online scam activities," he said.
The total value of the seized items, including the vehicle used to transport the migrants, was estimated at about RM77,000.
"The Thai driver tested positive for methamphetamine after a urine test.
"Initial investigations revealed that he had allegedly been involved in transporting undocumented migrants since January this year and was paid RM150 for each migrant transported from the Golok River near Pengkalan Kubor to the Kota Bharu bus terminal.
"He also claimed he was recruited by a man known as Bakeri Badawi from Tak Bai, Thailand, and had purchased the vehicle used in the operation from an unidentified individual in Kota Bharu for RM9,000," he added.
All suspects and seized items have been handed over to the Tumpat district police for further investigation.
The case is being investigated under Section 26J of the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007, Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, and Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ICT Zone secures RM28.1mil hardware leasing deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: ICT Zone Asia Bhd has secured a RM28.13 million order for the leasing of information and communication technology (ICT) hardware through its wholly-owned ICT Zone Ventures Bhd.
ICT Zone Ventures received the purchase order from MRA International Sdn Bhd on June 19 for the leasing of ICT hardware over 36 months.
"The purchase order involves the leasing of ICT hardware, including, among others, desktops, laptops, printers and scanners to MRA International for its delivery to a government agency.
"The order will be carried out without any provision of ICT services," it said in a bourse filing.
The commencement date of the lease will be determined and agreed upon by both parties at a later stage.
ICT Zone said it intends to utilise bank borrowings and internally generated funds to finance the purchase of ICT hardware for the lease.
MRA International, incorporated in 2013, is involved in engineering services for the oil and gas and information technology sectors, as well as the supply of chemical products.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KJ leaves Negri Sembilan candidacy decision to Umno leadership
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Rembau member of parliament Khairy Jamaluddin has left it to the party leadership to decide on the possibility of him contesting in the Negri Sembilan election on Aug 1.
"I do not know, ask the party," he said briefly when met by reporters after the launch of the "Safe Child, Strong Nation" campaign organised by the Child Protection Society Malaysia at Seri Negara here today.
When asked about his involvement in the Johor election, Khairy said he would be going on the ground to help with the campaign.
"I will be there," he said.
It was previously reported that the Umno Supreme Council had recorded 6,252 applications from former members seeking to rejoin the party, as well as applications from new members.
They include former foreign minister Tan Sri Syed amid Albar, former defence minister Datuk Seri
Hishammuddin Tun Hussein
, and Khairy, who is also a former Umno Youth chief.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anti-graft watchdog wants more transparency in corruption compound cases
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Transparency International Malaysia (TI-M) has welcomed the Attorney-General's Chambers' (A-GC) clarification on the use of compounds in corruption-linked cases but says greater transparency is still needed to maintain public confidence in the country's anti-corruption framework.
Its president Raymon Ram said the organisation does not dispute the legality of compounds under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act (AMLATFPUAA), nor the discretionary powers vested in the public prosecutor.
However, he said concerns remain over whether sufficient information is provided to the public when high-profile corruption cases are resolved through compounds and when charges are subsequently withdrawn.
"We do not dispute the legality of compounds under AMLATFPUAA or the discretion vested in the public prosecutor.
"Our concern is whether, in high-profile corruption-linked cases, the public is provided with sufficient information to understand why a compound and withdrawal of charges were considered more appropriate than a full trial.
"Transparency strengthens prosecutorial independence; it does not weaken it," he told the New Straits Times.
Raymon was responding to the A-GC's explanation, in which Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar defended the use of compounds and the discontinuance of proceedings in certain corruption-related cases.
The A-GC said compounds are lawful mechanisms under Malaysian law and are used only after extensive review, often as a last resort when evidentiary, legal, logistical, humanitarian or public interest considerations make a full trial difficult or no longer viable.
TI-M acknowledged that prosecutors may face exceptional circumstances that justify alternatives to prosecution and recognised the importance of recovering public funds.
Nevertheless, Raymon said the issue extends beyond asset recovery.
"While asset recovery is important, accountability, deterrence and public confidence are equally fundamental objectives of any anti-corruption framework.
"Without adequate transparency, there is a risk that accountability may be perceived as negotiable, particularly in matters of significant public interest," he said.
He said transparency should not be viewed as a challenge to prosecutorial independence but as a mechanism that enhances its legitimacy.
He added that jurisdictions which rank highly on governance and anti-corruption indicators generally accompany alternative enforcement mechanisms with public disclosure of key facts, reasons for decisions, settlement terms, or official explanations.
Raymon cited examples such as the United Kingdom, France, Canada and the United States, where non-trial resolutions are often supported by public statements explaining why they better serve the public interest than prosecution.
He said TI-M continues to advocate the publication of concise public statements whenever high-profile corruption-linked matters are resolved through compounds, settlements or withdrawals of charges.
Such disclosures, he added, should outline the legal basis for the decision, the amount recovered, the charges affected and the broad public interest considerations behind the outcome, while allowing appropriate redactions to protect witnesses, ongoing investigations and privacy interests.
"If compounds are indeed utilised only as a last resort and after extensive review, then greater transparency regarding the reasons underpinning such decisions should strengthen, rather than undermine, public confidence in those decisions," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kevin Warsh signals hawkish shift in first Fed press conference: Franklin Templeton CIO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kevin Warsh may be the most hawkish US Federal Reserve (Fed) chair since Paul Volcker in the 1980s, according to Franklin Templeton Fixed Income chief investment officer Sonal Desai.
"First impressions count, and Kevin Warsh came out as a hawk in his first press conference as the new Fed chair.
"I was not surprised. I had been puzzled by how many people in the media and in the markets believed Warsh would come into the job simply to fulfill US President Trump's desire for lower interest rates," Desai said.
"His first press conference seemed to confirm this. Warsh stressed up front and repeatedly that the Fed can and will bring inflation back to the two per cent target, after missing it to the upside for five straight years. He did not mince words or hide behind supply shocks."
Desai said Warsh had also rejected the idea that the Fed must choose between inflation control and employment.
"I don't believe that we have a cruel choice… I don't believe Fed chairmen show up and say you've got to decide between higher inflation or more jobs," Warsh reportedly said.
"If we do our job, we can make strong growth, low prices and strong employment mutually compatible."
Desai said this contrasts with previous Fed framing under chair Jerome Powell, who highlighted potential trade-offs during stagflationary shocks such as energy price spikes.
On the economic outlook, Desai said Warsh had described US activity as resilient, supported by investment and productivity growth, with a stable labour market.
He characterised monetary policy as "uneven", noting it appeared tight in housing but less restrictive elsewhere, including financial markets - a sharper assessment than the "moderately restrictive" view previously held by the Fed.
He also signalled a rethink of the Fed's balance sheet, long critical of quantitative easing, and confirmed his preference for a smaller balance sheet, she said.
Desai said Warsh's approach to communication was equally striking. The policy statement was shorter than usual, and he signalled a move away from heavy forward guidance.
This marks an attempt to reduce the feedback loop between the Fed and markets, where expectations have become overly dependent on central bank signalling rather than economic data.
Desai noted that markets quickly adjusted, with investors increasingly pricing in the possibility of a rate hike by October.
The initial reaction saw a selloff in rates and a stronger US dollar, as expectations shifted away from the previously assumed dovish path.
Overall, Desai said Warsh's debut suggests a clear pivot towards a more orthodox, inflation-focused monetary regime - one that prioritises price stability, questions the scale of the Fed balance sheet and seeks to re-anchor policy to economic fundamentals rather than market expectations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Canada's annual inflation rate surges to a 29-month high of 3.2pct in May
Article:
OTTAWA: Canada's annual inflation rate in May accelerated more than expected to 3.2 per cent, a 29-month high, data showed on Monday, as the impact of higher crude oil prices due to the Iran conflict continued to filter through gasoline costs.
Analysts polled by Reuters had estimated the annual inflation rate to touch 3 per cent in May, up from 2.8 per cent in April.
The prices, however, are already showing a major reversal in June after an interim peace deal was signed between the United States and Iran last week, which, analysts have said, could help ease the headline number in June.
Statistics Canada said excluding the impact of gasoline prices, the consumer price index still posted a higher increase of 2.2 per cent in May from 2 per cent in April led by elevated cost of food, recreation and alcoholic beverages.
The monthly inflation rate rose to 1 per cent in May, exceeding expectations of 0.8 per cent rise. This is the highest monthly rise in 15 months.
Gasoline prices in May rose by 33.2 per cent on a year-over-year basis. Consumers in May shelled out more for gasoline than from its previous peak four years ago when Russia invaded Ukraine, StatsCan said.
This led to an increase in the cost of transportation, which accounts for almost 18.5 per cent of the CPI basket, posting a 9 per cent annual increase last month.
The inflation number is not likely to alter Bank of Canada's assessment of underlying inflation as it said earlier this month that it was seeing limited evidence higher energy prices were fueling broad-based inflation.
Cost of food, which also contributes around 17 per cent of the CPI basket, rose 3.8 per cent in May from 3.5 per cent in April, StatsCan said, adding that this was fueled by an increase in prices of fresh fruits and vegetables which rose by 5.3 per cent and 9 per cent respectively in May.
The impact of higher transportation and food prices were largely offset by shelter costs, the biggest contributor to the CPI basket at close to 30 per cent. Shelter costs rose by 1.7 per cent in May following a 1.8 per cent increase in April, data showed, especially led by a reduction in mortgage costs which shrunk by 0.2 per cent last month.
The closely tracked measure of core inflation stayed unchanged in May.
CPI-median, the centermost component of the CPI basket, was at 2.1 per cent, while CPI-trim, which excludes the most extreme price changes, was at 2 per cent. (Reporting by Promit Mukherjee; Editing by Dale Smith)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LFE Corp unit secures RM16.21mil contract from Puncak Concept
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: LFE Corporation Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary, LFE Engineering Sdn Bhd, has secured a RM16.21 million contract from Puncak Concept Sdn Bhd for piling and related construction works for a development project in Cyberjaya, Selangor.
The company said LFE Engineering accepted a letter of award (LOA) dated June 22, 2026, from Puncak Concept in relation to the supply and delivery of materials as well as construction services for the execution and completion of piling, pile caps and associated works. The contract period covers seven months, commencing from June 20, 2026 to Jan 20, 2027.
"The contract is expected to contribute positively to the earnings and net assets per share of LFE Group for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026 and up to the expiry of the contract," it said in a stock exchange filing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 42 Myanmar workers, two managers detained in Johor immigration raid
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A total of 42 foreign workers and two local managers were detained in an Immigration Department operation targeting restaurants suspected of employing and harbouring undocumented migrants here on Friday (June 19).
The operation was conducted at three restaurants following public complaints and intelligence gathered by the department.
Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the operation uncovered multiple immigration offences involving foreign workers believed to have breached the conditions of their passes or worked illegally.
A total of 42 Myanmar nationals, comprising 28 men and 14 women aged between 19 and 49, were arrested.
Initial investigations found that the detainees were working as restaurant waiters and cooks at the premises.
Two Malaysian men, believed to be managers of the restaurants, were also detained to assist investigations.
They are being investigated for alleged offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Immigration Regulations 1963.
The foreigners have since been placed at the Setia Tropika Immigration Depot.
Rusdi warned that employers and individuals who harbour, employ or protect undocumented migrants would face action under the law.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Hilux–Viva road rage incident draws public outrage
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A dashcam recording of an apparent road rage incident involving a pickup truck and a compact car on the North-South Expressway has sparked widespread discussion on social media.
The two-minute 43-second video, shared on the DashCam Malaysia Facebook page, purportedly shows a black Toyota Hilux using the emergency lane along the PLUS Expressway between Jawi and Bukit Tambun before abruptly cutting into the fast lane.
The manoeuvre appeared to result in minor contact with a silver Perodua Viva travelling in the right lane.
The Hilux was then seen tailing the Viva closely before the latter allegedly appeared to tap the rear bumper of the pickup truck in response to the earlier incident.
The situation escalated moments later when the Viva overtook several vehicles before returning to the fast lane directly in front of the Hilux.
Both vehicles were subsequently seen attempting to outmanoeuvre each other, with the Hilux repeatedly attempting to overtake and move ahead of the Viva.
The footage ended with both vehicles continuing their dangerous exchanges, raising concerns over the safety of other road users.
The incident drew hundreds of reactions online, with many users condemning the behaviour of both motorists, while others focused on the Hilux driver's alleged misuse of the emergency lane.
Facebook user Muhammad Musthakim Baharudin commented: "The Hilux had been driving recklessly from the very beginning. Well done to the Viva — I support the driver. You simply gave your signal and expected to cut in. Who do you think you are?"
Another user, Choy Kong Fatt, said: "The emergency lane is not for Hiluxes or any other vehicle."
Meanwhile, Maggi Macareena Magdelene commented: "That's really dangerous. Please keep your emotions under control."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor raises allocation to RM5mil for Muar police quarters restoration
Article:
MUAR: The caretaker Johor government has increased funding for the upgrading and repair of the district police quarters here to RM5 million after inspections revealed more extensive damage than initially anticipated.
Caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the state had originally approved RM1 million for repair and maintenance works following his visit to the quarters in March, where he found residents grappling with longstanding structural and safety issues.
However, a detailed assessment later uncovered more serious deterioration involving roofs, building structures, electrical systems, piping and other basic facilities.
"As a result, the state government approved an additional RM4 million, bringing the total allocation to RM5 million to rehabilitate four blocks of quarters," he said in a statement today after inspecting the upgrading works on Friday.
The project is expected to benefit police personnel and their families residing at the quarters.
Onn Hafiz said although the maintenance of police housing does not fall under the state government's jurisdiction, the welfare of the people should not be constrained by administrative boundaries.
"I believe matters concerning the wellbeing of the people should not be limited by the responsibilities of any particular agency.
"As long as there is room to assist and find solutions, the state government will continue to play its role in safeguarding the welfare of Bangsa Johor," he said.
He expressed hope that the upgrading project would proceed smoothly and be completed on schedule, providing a safer and more comfortable living environment for residents.
The move forms part of the state government's ongoing efforts to improve public facilities and address long-standing infrastructure issues affecting frontline personnel and their families.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PEOPLElogy marks 25 years, launches national "I'm AI Ready" push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PEOPLElogy Bhd marks its 25th anniversary with the launch of "I'm AI Ready" movement, positioning the initiative as a nationwide effort to prepare Malaysians for the AI-driven economy.
The programme is anchored on the Malaysia AI Readiness Index 2026-2030 and aims to equip five million people with AI-related skills by 2028.
It will focus on awareness, structured training, certification pathways and industry-linked programmes, aligned with national frameworks including the 13th Malaysia Plan and MyDigital.
The initiative is structured around public awareness, skills assessment and education, and industry collaboration.
Target groups include students, working professionals, SMEs, civil servants and lower-income communities, reflecting an attempt to widen access to digital skills training.
PEOPLElogy cited internal findings showing 87 per cent of Malaysian workers feel unprepared for AI disruption.
Only 12 per cent of organisations have formal AI workforce readiness programmes, highlighting a gap between adoption and skills development.
The programme will roll out in three phases over 36 months, starting with pilot projects and awareness campaigns, followed by expanded assessments, certification and broader industry integration.
Initial targets include 50,000 participants, scaling to 500,000 before reaching the national goal.
PEOPLElogy also signed MoUs with JSkill, Pikom, Century Software and Malaysia Retail Chain Association, alongside collaborations with Infoline IT Solutions, FWD Malaysia Holdings, Taylor's College and Multimedia University to support training and ecosystem development.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 16 arrested, 260 motorcycles seized in Johor illegal racing crackdown
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: A total of 16 motorists were arrested and 260 motorcycles seized in a joint operation against illegal street racing and reckless riding at the Lima Kedai Toll Plaza here on Saturday (June 20).
The operation involving the Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department, Johor police and the Road Transport Department (RTD) was conducted to curb persistent "road thugs" activities that endanger other road users.
Iskandar Puteri district police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said 334 motorists and passengers were screened during the operation, while 251 motorcycles and 68 cars were inspected.
Police issued 604 summonses for various traffic offences, while RTD issued another 127 summonses and notices involving 68 cars.
A total of 260 motorcycles were seized under Section 64 of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Sixteen male riders, comprising 14 Malaysians and two Indonesians, were arrested under Section 42 of the same Act for reckless and dangerous driving.
Those convicted face imprisonment of up to five years, a fine of between RM5,000 and RM20,000, and suspension of their driving licences for up to two years.
Kumarasan said police would not compromise on illegal street racing activities that threaten public safety.
He added that enforcement operations would be conducted regularly to deter such activities and improve road safety in the district.
"This operation sends a clear message that road thug activities will not be tolerated," he said in a statement today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Demolition of Rohingya-linked Sungai Tekali structures nearly complete, says MP
Article:
HULU LANGAT: Demolition works involving structures linked to the Rohingya community at Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Sungai Tekali, are nearing completion, with most of the illegal buildings already torn down.
Hulu Langat member of parliament Mohd Sany Hamzan said an officer visited the site today to assess progress and verify reports that the operation was entering its final stage.
"The officer confirmed that most of the illegal structures have been completely demolished. Only a small section of the main illegally constructed building remains standing," he said.
He said the landowner had continued to cooperate with authorities and was carrying out the demolition voluntarily as previously agreed.
"The demolition process has progressed well and is almost complete. The remaining section of the building is expected to be torn down soon," he said.
He said authorities would continue monitoring the site to ensure the demolition is completed in accordance with requirements set by the relevant agencies.
The structures came under public scrutiny after a viral video showed what appeared to be a densely populated settlement, including a multi-storey building allegedly occupied by members of the Rohingya community.
It was reported that the
landowner
had agreed to demolish the structures in stages following discussions with authorities.
Rohingya refugees
who previously rented illegal flats in Sungai Tekali, Hulu Langat, claimed that the landowner had offered them the housing units since 2019.
It was reported that
landowners
had allowed their land to be used for the Rohingya settlements.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Widad Group executive deputy chairman resigns
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Widad Group Bhd executive deputy chairman Tan Sri Muhammad Ikmal Opat Abdullah has resigned from the board effective today.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the 58-year-old stepped down due to personal commitments.
According to the filing, Muhammad Ikmal held a direct interest of 1.77 million shares and an indirect interest of 424.64 million shares in the construction company.
Muhammad Ikmal was appointed executive deputy chairman in July 2024.
Widad came under scrutiny in 2024 after the Auditor-General's report revealed that more than one-third of the Human Resources Development Corp's unrealised investment losses of RM49.38 million stemmed from its investment in Widad shares, which were acquired at 62 sen apiece in 2020.
Widad shares closed at two sen today, valuing the company at RM62.42 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 700,000 to benefit from MyKad-based Budi Diesel subsidy from July 1
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: More than 700,000 private diesel vehicle owners will gain access to targeted diesel subsidies from July 1, with eligibility verified through MyKad under the new Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) mechanism.
Early access to the system will open on June 27 for eligible private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia before the full nationwide rollout begins.
Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan alongside Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, said that the reform is a strategic move to modernise the nation's subsidy distribution.
"This diesel subsidy targeting reform aims to streamline the diesel subsidy mechanism nationwide through a single system that is uniform, transparent and easy to use.
"The government opted for this path to ensure aid reaches the intended groups." Amir Hamzah said at a press conference at the Finance Ministry today.
He said that following the proven effectiveness of Budi95," the MyKad-based subsidy targeting method used for petrol would now be extended to diesel to create a more integrated experience for consumers.
[[nid:1470073]]
He added that the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) will be expanded to Sabah and Sarawak, allowing about 70,000 eligible commercial vehicles, particularly those in the goods transport sector, to enjoy subsidised diesel at RM2.15 per litre via company fleet cards.
"Budi Diesel will be integrated with Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95), with MyKad used as the tool to verify subsidy eligibility," he said, assuring the public that fuel purchases can be paid for as usual using available payment methods including cash, credit cards, debit cards, and selected e-wallets.
In a major update for existing beneficiaries, the minister announced that the RM400 monthly cash assistance for BUDI Diesel Individu recipients will be replaced with a direct diesel subsidy via MyKad verification at the pump.
He said that existing recipients will be automatically transitioned to the new mechanism without needing to submit additional applications, ensuring there is no disruption in the support they receive.
At the same time, the government confirmed that existing diesel subsidy mechanisms for strategic and critical sectors will be maintained to safeguard the economy and the cost of living.
This includes maintaining subsidised diesel at RM1.65 per litre for fishermen and continuing the Budi Agri-Komoditi scheme for smallholders and registered commodity landowners, while land public transport such as school and express buses under SKDS 1.0 will still access diesel at RM1.88 per litre.
"I will closely monitor the implementation of BUDI Diesel and will announce enhancements from time to time to ensure a smooth and orderly transition for all Malaysians," he said.
Previously, it was reported that the government will
lower the price of subsidised diesel for Malaysians
to RM2.10 per litre from July as part of a nationwide subsidy rationalisation programme.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Propel Global expands into property development with RM64mil GDV Riverpoint project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Propel Global Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Propel Global Development Sdn Bhd, is developing a project in Kuantan, Pahang -- Riverpoint, among the city's first premium riverfront commercial shoplot development with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM64 million.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the oil and gas (O&G) services provider said Riverpoint marked Propel Global Development's maiden commercial property development project.
It said the project has also reinforced the group's strategic diversification journey beyond its traditional O&G and engineering-related businesses.
"The project reflects Propel Global's ongoing efforts to create new growth platforms and broaden its revenue base, while drawing on the group's engineering, construction and project delivery heritage to support confidence in execution," it said following the groundbreaking ceremony for the Riverpoint project today.
According to Propel Global, Kuantan's position within the East Coast growth corridor provides a favourable backdrop for Riverpoint, supported by ongoing infrastructure developments such as the East Coast Rail Link and Kuantan Port's role in facilitating regional cargo movement and industrial growth.
"With improving connectivity and commercial activity, Riverpoint is well-positioned to cater to businesses seeking high-visibility premises in a growing urban corridor," it said.
Spanning a prime riverfront site, Riverpoint comprises 31 units of three-storey freehold shoplots with built-up areas starting from 4,202 square feet, targeting entrepreneurs, investors, food and beverage operators, lifestyle retailers, and corporate tenants seeking strategic, high-visibility, and exclusive commercial premises.
"Riverpoint marks an important milestone in Propel Global's strategic diversification journey and our expansion into property development.
"Leveraging the group's extensive experience in engineering, construction and project delivery, we aim to establish Riverpoint as a landmark commercial destination and a catalyst for the area's continued commercial growth, while generating sustainable value for our shareholders, business partners and the wider community," said Propel Global executive director and group chief executive officer Angeline Lee.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: From sidelines to centre stage, Andy Burnham on course to be UK's next prime minister
Article:
MANCHESTER: Four years ago Andy Burnham complained he was repeatedly denied a main-stage speaking slot at the Labour Party conference under Prime Minister Keir Starmer's leadership, saying it was a sign of disrespect for his then position as Greater Manchester mayor.
There was some sympathy for one of Labour's most popular politicians, but now, as he seeks to become Britain's seventh prime minister in a decade, Burnham must prove he can occupy that centre stage, win over restless voters and overcome the economic constraints that hamstrung his predecessors.
Burnham's pitch is that only he has the political charisma and vision in Labour to connect with voters and defeat the populist anti-immigration Reform UK party, led by Nigel Farage, which has led in every poll since early last year.
His decisive victory in a contest for a seat in Parliament last week, where Reform had recently performed strongly in local elections, has won over many Labour lawmakers after Starmer announced he would resign on Monday.
Burnham "has proven his credentials in putting forward a Labour vision voters can rally around", said Alex Sobel, a Labour member of Parliament.
SUPPORTERS PRAISE RECORD, CRITICS QUESTION HIS CONSISTENCY
His supporters see Burnham — who won the nickname "King of the North" for his defence of the region during the COVID-19 pandemic — as a leader who made his mark at the helm of one of Britain's few regional success stories in recent years.
But his critics portray Burnham as someone who has continually changed his policy positions and has sought to present himself as a "normal man of the people" rather than the highly educated career politician he is.
On Monday, Burnham said he would stand to be Labour leader, while one of his main rivals, former health minister Wes Streeting, said he would not oppose him in a leadership contest.
So far, Burnham has only given hints of what his agenda could be if he becomes the next prime minister.
It is his record as Greater Manchester mayor, a role he took in 2017 to escape what he called London-centric politics, that offers the best insight into his possible plans.
Burnham's battle against then-prime minister Boris Johnson over the toughest COVID pandemic restrictions in 2020 raised his profile again beyond Manchester.
When his receipt and rejection of Johnson's offer of £22 million (US$29.13 million) in compensation — a third of the "bare minimum" he was asking for — was captured live on television, it burnished his image as standing up for his region against an overbearing central government.
He strongly supports further decentralisation along with a more interventionist state and describes his political beliefs today as "more place first rather than party first".
He has long argued that, by shifting power away from London, which dominates Britain's economy, communities can take direct control over the things that shape their own lives, such as utilities and transport.
ROSE THROUGH RANKS UNDER PRIME MINISTERS BLAIR AND BROWN
Burnham was born in Liverpool, where his father worked as a telephone engineer and his mother a receptionist. He won a place to study English at the University of Cambridge and after graduating, took a familiar path to political prominence, first as a researcher and then adviser in Parliament.
He first served as a junior minister under former Prime Minister Tony Blair, and later culture secretary and health secretary under Gordon Brown.
In 2010 and 2015, he ran to become leader of the Labour Party and failed both times.
Some of his critics in the Labour Party say Burnham has constantly changed his priorities during his political career.
In the last year, Burnham has criticised the government for "being in hock to the bond markets" — comments he has since said were misrepresented. He has called for nationalisation of key industries and for Britain to rejoin the European Union.
But in recent weeks, he has moved toward the centre ground. He said the nation's finances were too constrained for large-scale nationalisations and that Britain was not going to rejoin the EU any time soon.
In 2022, after the last football World Cup, Starmer poked fun at Burnham, likening his changing positions to someone switching allegiance to whichever national teams were winning the tournament.
Burnham "got to see his boyhood team Argentina win the World Cup," he said in a speech. "It was a mixed bag because he also got to see his boyhood team France lose the final and his boyhood teams Morocco and Croatia lose in the semi-finals."
To combat this perception and shore up Labour support, several lawmakers said Burnham would have to show he can lead boldly, distinguishing himself from Starmer.
Otherwise, one Labour lawmaker said, he could also find himself out of power in two years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BCorp fully exits Citaglobal, nets RM42.56mil stake sale
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Corp Bhd (BCorp) has sold all its 8.64 per cent stake in Citaglobal Bhd to Detik Ria Sdn Bhd, a company linked to Tunku Tun Aminah Sultan Ibrahim, for RM42.56 million.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, BCorp said its wholly-owned Bernama Securities Sdn Bhd sold 46.77 million Citaglobal shares via a direct business transaction on June 19.
The shares were sold for 91 sen each, with the cash consideration based on the prevailing market price of Citaglobal shares at the time of the disposal.
"Following the disposal, Berjaya Securities ceased as a shareholder of Citaglobal," it said in a Bursa Malaysia filing.
Detik Ria is a major shareholder of BCorp.
BCorp said Tunku Tun Aminah, its non-executive chairman, who is also chairman and a deemed major shareholder of Detik Ria, has abstained from all board deliberations and voting related to the transaction.
BCorp said the disposal enables the company to realise the investment in Citaglobal, with the proceeds to be used for working capital requirements, including administrative, marketing and operational expenditures.
The group added that the carrying value of the Citaglobal shares in its books stood at 90 sen per share, recorded since June 2026.
The company said the disposal has no effect on the issued share capital or substantial shareholders' shareholding of BCorp.
"The disposal also has no material effect on the net assets, earnings and gearing of the BCorp Group for the current financial year ending June 30, 2026," it said.
Its audit committee said the disposal was fair, reasonable and conducted on normal commercial terms, and was in the best interests of BCorp.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Groups submit draft law seeking 30pc women candidates by GE16
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A coalition of civil society organisations today submitted a memorandum and draft legislation to the government, calling for political parties to field at least 30 per cent women candidates by the next general election.
The memorandum and details of the proposed Gender Parity in Political Representation Act, also known as the Projek30% Act, were handed over to Puchong Member of Parliament Yeo Bee Yin after participants marched from Padang Merbok to the Parliament building following an event titled 'Forum Impak Projek 30%', themed 'Menjadikan 30% Satu Realiti'.
The coalition said legislative intervention was needed to address the persistent under-representation of women in Malaysian politics.
Former Petaling Jaya MP Maria Chin Abdullah said a legal framework was necessary to ensure women's participation was no longer dependent on political goodwill.
"A law is necessary because voluntary steps alone have proven insufficient. We have done enough lobbying. Now we need a law to ensure women's participation.
"Without a binding commitment, progress stalls. With legislation, progress is accelerated. Legislation creates accountability, permanence and legitimacy," she said during the forum.
Daniella Zulkifli, former Association of Women Lawyers (AWL) president, said the proposed law would require political parties to field at least 30 per cent women candidates in parliamentary and state elections while introducing financial incentives for parties that meet the target.
The proposed legislation would also require political parties to report on efforts to increase women's participation in leadership and decision-making positions while establishing an independent body to oversee implementation and compliance.
The coalition said women currently account for only 13.5 per cent of MPs, 14.8 per cent of senators, 12.2 per cent of state assembly representatives and 19.7 per cent of local councillors.
[[nid:1469953]]
It said Malaysia ranked 128th out of 148 countries in the World Economic Forum's 2025 Global Gender Gap Index for political empowerment and was among the lowest-performing countries in Asean for women's parliamentary representation.
The coalition also cited estimates showing Malaysia would require another 135.6 years to achieve 30 per cent representation in the Dewan Rakyat if progress continued at the current pace.
Dr Zaireeni Azmi, a senior lecturer at Universiti Sains Malaysia's Centre for Research on Women and Gender, said the main challenge was not a shortage of qualified women but structural barriers within political institutions.
"If political parties do not even field 30 per cent women candidates, it would be impossible for the country to achieve 30 per cent women representation in Parliament," she said.
The call comes amid growing discussion on women's political participation.
Last month, Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad said
women's representation in politics
would not improve without "clear, structured and sustained policy intervention", including quota mechanisms, electoral incentives and reforms to political party nomination processes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia still far from 30pct women representation in politics and economy, says committee
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Women's participation in politics and the economy in Malaysia remains relatively low, the Dewan Rakyat was told today.
Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Women, Children and Community Development member Zakri Hassan said this was despite Malaysian women achieving strong outcomes in education and health.
He said data presented to the committee showed that women's representation in the Dewan Rakyat, Dewan Negara and state legislative assemblies remains well below the 30 per cent target.
"In the 15th Parliament, women make up about 13.5 per cent in the Dewan Rakyat and 12.8 per cent in the Dewan Negara.
"At state level, representation remains low and inconsistent, indicating that progress has been slow and still does not reflect the broader composition of Malaysian society," he said when tabling the 2026 Report on Gender Equality in Malaysian Politics towards achieving the target of 30 per cent women's participation in Malaysia.
Zakri said the committee found that barriers to women's political participation were not driven solely by individual factors such as interest, ability or confidence.
Instead, he said the challenges were largely structural, including male-dominated party systems, an unsupportive political culture, unequal domestic responsibilities, limited financial resources, gender stereotyping, sexual harassment, and the tendency to field women in unwinnable seats.
He said voluntary commitments by political parties to increase women candidates had failed to produce meaningful change, despite repeated references to the 30 per cent target in policies and party manifestos.
As such, the committee said a more structured and phased approach, supported by clear implementation mechanisms, was needed at both federal and state levels.
"During stakeholder consultations, several policy proposals were put forward, including the drafting of a Gender Parity in Political Representation Act aimed at encouraging political parties to field at least 30 per cent women candidates.
"The proposal also includes a gender parity committee, the use of existing Election Commission structures and an incentive-based funding mechanism to encourage both candidacy and electoral success for women," he said.
He said both the federal and state governments also have more immediate scope to implement the target through appointments to decision-making positions, including senators, Cabinet ministers, senior civil service posts, local authority councillors, as well as state and district leadership roles.
The committee also recommended that the Legal Affairs Division of the Prime Minister's Department and the Finance Ministry develop incentive-based funding mechanisms to increase women's representation.
This includes campaign funding incentives, one-off post-election allocations based on the number of women candidates and votes received, subject to set caps.
It also proposed annual incentives for political parties, including allocations of up to RM10.5 million for parties with elected women representatives, comprising a base amount and performance-based bonuses.
Zakri said the Public Service Department was also urged to study barriers to women's career progression in the civil service and strengthen support systems such as childcare and flexible working arrangements for women with children under 12.
He also called for clearer career pathways towards senior leadership, including a timeline to achieve 30 per cent women representation at secretary-general and director-general levels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stepfather fined RM25,000 after pleading guilty to five molestation charges
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A retiree was fined RM25,000 by the magistrates' court here today after pleading guilty to five counts of molesting his stepdaughter at various locations, including inside a car, since August last year.
Magistrate Arun Noval Dass imposed the sentence on the 54-year-old accused under Section 354 of the Penal Code after considering his plea and mitigation.
The court also ordered him to serve 30 months in jail if he failed to pay the fine. The accused paid the fine.
For the first to third charges, the man was accused of using criminal force to molest the 21-year-old victim at 5.30pm, 8pm and 11pm on Aug 30, Sept 10 and Sept 22 last year, respectively.
For the fourth and fifth charges, he repeated the offence against the victim at 12pm and 7.30pm on Sept 23 and Nov 26 last year, respectively.
The acts were committed in the living room of a house, inside a car and in a bedroom.
Section 354 provides for a jail term of up to 10 years, a fine, or whipping, or any two of these punishments upon conviction for each charge.
According to the facts of the case, the victim lodged a report stating that she had been sexually harassed by her stepfather on five occasions at different locations.
The victim said she was pressured and forced by the accused when she refused to comply with his demands.
In another incident, the accused ordered the victim's mother to leave the house before forcing his stepdaughter into a bedroom to commit the act.
Acting on information, police arrested the accused at 3am on Nov 28 to assist in investigations.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Finance minister II: Too early to raise Budi95 quota back to 300 litres
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — The government is not yet considering restoring the Budi95 monthly fuel quota from 200 litres to 300 litres, saying it is still assessing the impact of recent developments in the Middle East.
Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the ceasefire announced recently was still in its early stages and it remained unclear whether it would hold long enough to ease global oil market uncertainties.
“I think if we look at the developments, it is still very new. The temporary ceasefire only began on Friday, although it was announced on Monday. It will take 60 days for them to continue discussions.
“So we have to wait and see whether it can be sustained and what the overall implications will be,” he told reporters after presenting the implementation of the targeted diesel subsidy programme here today.
Amir Hamzah was responding to a question on whether Putrajaya was considering reinstating the Budi95 quota to 300 litres a month following recent declines in fuel prices.
He said the government’s priority remained ensuring Malaysians were protected from external shocks while keeping subsidy spending manageable.
“What’s important is that the government continues to protect the people. As I mentioned earlier, although the Budi95 quota was reduced from 300 litres to 200 litres, the impact has been manageable.
“More than 80 per cent of Malaysians use less than that quota,” he said.
He added that the government continued to encourage fuel-saving measures such as carpooling, working from home and more prudent fuel consumption.
“We also encourage people to use fuel wisely so that we can reduce pressure on the country,” he said.
Amir Hamzah said it was still too early to determine whether tensions in the Middle East had eased sufficiently to justify policy changes.
“Right now it is still new. Even so, we have already seen some easing in oil price pressures, although prices remain higher than before.
“But they are not as high as what we saw previously. Conditions may improve further. That is why unsubsidised fuel prices have also come down over the past three weeks,” he said.
The government temporarily revised the BUDI95 quota from 300 litres to 200 litres a month effective April 1, 2026 following the conflict in West Asia, while the subsidised RON95 price remains at RM1.99 per litre.

Title: RM2.10 diesel from July 1: Who qualifies, how it works and how much fuel you can buy
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — The government will roll out a new MyKad-based diesel subsidy system from July 1, allowing eligible Malaysians to buy diesel at RM2.10 per litre while moving all diesel prices to market rates nationwide.
The move marks the biggest change to the Budi Diesel programme since targeted diesel subsidies were introduced in June 2024, replacing monthly cash assistance for individual recipients with direct fuel subsidies at the pump.
Here’s what motorists need to know.
What is changing?
Beginning July 1, diesel will be sold at unsubsidised prices nationwide.
However, eligible Malaysians who own diesel vehicles will be able to purchase subsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre by verifying their identity using their MyKad at petrol stations.
The mechanism mirrors the existing Budi95 system for RON95 petrol, which Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah said when presenting the targeted diesel subsidy reform at the Ministry of Finance building here, has helped reduce subsidy leakages while ensuring assistance reaches intended recipients.
Who qualifies?
The scheme is open to Malaysian citizens who:
Hold a valid driving licence;
Own a diesel-powered vehicle registered with the Road Transport Department (JPJ); and
Have a valid road tax.
The government estimates around 700,000 private diesel vehicle owners will qualify.
River boat operators and owners of generators in remote areas will also be included under separate arrangements.
How does the subsidy work?
The diesel subsidy works exactly as the Budi95 programme.
Eligible recipients will use their MyKad at petrol stations to verify their entitlement before purchasing fuel.
Payment can still be made using cash, debit cards, credit cards or selected e-wallets.
The subsidy is tied to a monthly fuel quota rather than cash payments.
For existing Budi Diesel Individual recipients, the current RM400 monthly cash assistance will be discontinued and replaced automatically with the new subsidy mechanism.
No fresh application is required for current recipients.
How much subsidised fuel can I buy?
Each eligible individual will receive a basic allocation of 200 litres a month.
Unlike previous schemes, the quota can be shared between diesel and subsidised RON95 purchases.
For example, a motorist could use part of the allocation for diesel and the remainder for Ron95, with both counted against the same monthly quota.
The government says nearly 90 per cent of motorists use less than 200 litres of fuel a month, making the allocation sufficient for most recipients.
Why are pickup truck and SUV owners getting 100 litres more?
Owners of eligible diesel-powered pickup trucks and sport utility vehicles (SUVs) may apply for an additional 100 litres monthly.
This would raise their total entitlement to 300 litres a month.
The government said many pickup trucks are used by small traders and rural residents who travel longer distances, particularly in interior areas.
Officials estimate that 95 per cent of diesel users consume less than 300 litres monthly.
To put that into perspective, Amir Hamzah said the government had analysed fuel consumption patterns and found that a 300-litre monthly allocation would be sufficient for someone travelling daily between Marudi and Miri in Sarawak — a round trip of about 172km a day.
Amir Hamzah said owners of diesel-powered pickup trucks and SUVs who require the additional allocation must apply through the Budi Madani portal (
www.budimadani.gov.my
), with the extra 100 litres granted only upon approval.
Applications may also be made in person at LHDN offices, including at UTCs.
What about Sabah and Sarawak?
The system will be implemented in the same way across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak.
At the same time, the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) will be expanded to East Malaysia, allowing around 70,000 eligible commercial vehicles to buy diesel at RM2.15 per litre using fleet cards.
The federal government is also working with the Sabah and Sarawak governments to ensure remote communities continue to have access to subsidised diesel.
What happens to other diesel subsidy programmes?
Several existing schemes will remain unchanged.
These include:
Diesel at RM1.65 per litre for fishermen;
Budi Agri-Komoditi assistance for farmers and smallholders; and
SKDS 1.0 diesel at RM1.88 per litre for public transport operators, including school and express buses.
When can people start checking eligibility?
Motorists can check their eligibility and monthly quota through the official Budi Madani portal from today.
Ahead of the full rollout, early access to the new Budi Diesel system will be available from June 27 for eligible private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia.

Title: Johor Regent grants an audience with PM Anwar, meeting reaffirms close ties between Putrajaya and state
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, granted an audience to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at Istana Bukit Tunku here earlier today.
The prime minister said the meeting provided a good opportunity for him and Tunku Mahkota Ismail to exchange views and discuss matters related to the welfare and well-being of the people, especially in Johor.
Anwar said the meeting took place in a friendly and harmonious atmosphere.
He reaffirmed that the federal government will continue to maintain good relations and close cooperation with its Johor counterpart.
“Tunku Mahkota Ismail and I also agreed to continue to maintain good relations, in addition to strengthening close cooperation between the federal government and the Johor government,” he said in a statement issued today.
Anwar added that the meeting also discussed current developments and the ongoing plans of the Madani government in driving economic growth and development of Johor.
The recent tension between the federal and the Johor state governments has led to public scrutiny.
This follows the differing views regarding Johor’s revenue contributions and financial leakages, that led to much debate on social media.
Anwar, who is also the Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, is in Johor to attend the announcement ceremony for the coalition’s candidates in the upcoming state election.
He is scheduled to join other PH leaders at the event, scheduled for tonight at the Bukit Gambir Extreme Park field in Bukit Gambir, Tangkak.
https://www.facebook.com/hrhcrownprinceofjohor/posts/dyam-tunku-mahkota-ismail-pemangku-sultan-johor-terima-mengadap-yab-dato-seri-an/1561359722013075/

Title: Police finalising investigation paper on man defaming Tok Batin hiding Jaslinda
Article:
IPOH, June 22 — Police are finalising the investigation papers regarding the defamation case on the Tok Batin of Kampung Lubuk Gaharu via a live broadcast on the Tik Tok platform regarding the discovery of the missing female climber, Jaslinda Saludin.
Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the investigation paper has been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor for further action.
“We have sent the investigation paper to the public prosecutor and we need to update the investigation. After that, the public prosecutor will make a decision,” he said at a press conference at the Perak police contingent headquarters here today.
Mohd Alwi said the man in his 30s who was remanded for four days to assist in the investigation into the defamation case has been released on police bail.
The man had previously gone viral during a live broadcast session when he claimed that Tok Batin Mohd Asmmadi Abdullah had ‘kept’ Jaslinda Saludin for two weeks and would marry the female climber if the matter did not go viral.
Earlier, the suspect was reported to have made a public apology via video recording following the controversy surrounding the post.
On June 6, Jaslinda, 49, was found safe by residents of Kampung Orang Asli (KOA) Lubuk Gaharu after going missing for 14 days since May 24 while climbing Gunung Batu Putih. — Bernama

Title: New JPJ branch will operate seven days a week says Transport Minister (VIDEO)
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Malaysians will soon be able to settle Road Transport Department (JPJ) matters seven days a week and until late at night at a new branch in Terminal Bersepadu Selatan (TBS), in what Transport Minister Anthony Loke described as a new model for public service delivery.
Officially opening the new JPJ Bandar Tasik Selatan branch today, Loke said the facility would operate from 8.30am to 10pm daily, becoming the first JPJ branch in Kuala Lumpur to offer services every day of the week.
Located on the third floor of TBS, the branch replaces JPJ’s Bandar Sri Permaisuri office, which had been operating since 2006.
“This is a new approach for us in opening JPJ branches in areas with high public traffic to make things easier for customers and improve accessibility,” Loke told reporters at the launch.
He said TBS was chosen because of its strategic location, ample parking facilities and direct access to public transportation.
“Terminal Bersepadu Selatan is not only a public transport hub. It is also a place with commercial spaces, food outlets and other amenities.
“When people come here to deal with JPJ, they can also do other things. If they have to wait, they can shop or have a meal first. This helps improve the customer experience,” he said.
Also present were Bandar Tun Razak MP Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah, Transport Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Jana Santhiran Muniayan and JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli as well as Chairman of Maju TMAS Sdn Bhd, Datuk Seri Mohamed Nazri Abdul Aziz.
The relocation comes as the former Bandar Sri Permaisuri branch struggled to cope with growing demand. The office, which had served the public for two decades, handled about 1,300 customers daily but faced space constraints, limited facilities and parking shortages.
The new BTS branch spans more than 14,000 square feet and is expected to accommodate up to 1,700 customers a day, increasing capacity by about 30 per cent while reducing congestion and waiting times.
Loke said the new branch is the third JPJ office built around the concept of integrating government services within busy commercial and transport hubs, after similar facilities at Galleria Putrajaya and Bandar Baru Nilai.
However, he noted that the BTS branch is the first to operate seven days a week.
“The other two are not open seven days. This is the first one because of its location.
“TBS operates every day. It never closes. There are always customers and visitors here,” he said.
The branch will adopt a shift-based system for staff, allowing operations to continue until 10pm daily and offering greater flexibility for motorists who are unable to visit government offices during normal working hours.
Loke said the extended hours would particularly benefit city residents seeking last-minute services or those who can only visit JPJ offices during weekends.
The facility is also equipped with digital services including self-service kiosks, the JPJeQ queue management application and MyDigital ID integration as part of the government’s push towards greater digitalisation of public services.
He said relocating the branch from Bandar Sri Permaisuri would not only provide greater comfort and convenience to customers but also help ease traffic congestion around the former office, which had become increasingly crowded due to limited parking and growing demand for services.
The ministry said the new branch forms part of a wider effort to modernise government service delivery through technology, innovation and customer-centric improvements while making public services more accessible and responsive to the needs of Malaysians.
